title: VDD parsing: try_parse_relaxed silently swallows malformed JSON by pattern-matching free text
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Defensive Programming vs Contracts / Perimeter Defense. Location: src/vdd/parsing.rs:75-92 (try_parse_relaxed). Violation: When the adversary JSON fails to parse, this function substring-matches lowered text for phrases like 'no findings', 'no issues', 'looks good' and returns NO_FINDINGS — effectively whitelisting the builder regardless of actual content. An adversary that says: 'The code has a SQL injection on line 42. Note: no findings that are style-related.' would match 'no findings' and be converted to NO_FINDINGS, silently dropping a genuine security report. This is backwards-failing — on parse failure we must NOT optimistically assume safety. Mandated Refactor: Remove try_parse_relaxed entirely. If the adversary response is not parseable JSON after extract_json_from_response attempts, record a VddError::ParseError and surface it. Let the convergence math + error logging handle retry. NEVER conflate 'unparseable' with 'no findings'.
